_doc: REINS-Bench corpus compiled from §1.5.2 yaml prompts. W_fix 30 + W_scaffold 20 = 50 entries. Compatible with T1.b/T1.d/T2.a/T2.b CorpusEntry loader.

items.title: off-by-one in pagination cursor
items.prompt:
The /api/users endpoint returns 21 records when limit=20 because the cursor
decrement happens after the slice. Find the bug, fix it, add a regression
test, and run the test suite.

items.title: race condition in counter increment
items.prompt:
In `app/services/counter.py`, the `increment` method reads, mutates,
and writes a shared dict without locking, so concurrent calls drop
updates under load. Identify the race, fix it (a lock, an atomic
primitive, or a redesign — your call), add a regression test that
spins up multiple threads and asserts the final count, and run the
test suite.

items.title: timezone-naive datetime in API response
items.prompt:
The `/api/events` endpoint serialises `event.created_at` with
`datetime.utcnow()` and no timezone info, so clients in non-UTC
zones display events one offset off. Make the timestamps explicitly
UTC-aware on the wire (ISO-8601 with `Z` or `+00:00`), add a test
that pins the JSON shape, and run the test suite.

items.title: SQL N+1 in user-with-orders endpoint
items.prompt:
`/api/users/<id>/orders` issues one query for the user and then one
query per order to load each line item, producing N+1 queries on a
large account. Refactor the loader to fetch line items in a single
joined query (or a `selectinload` on the relationship), add a
regression test that asserts the query count is bounded, and run the
suite.

items.title: file handle leak in CSV importer
items.prompt:
`app/importers/csv_importer.py` opens the source file with `open()`
but never closes it on the early-return error paths, so long-running
processes leak descriptors and eventually hit `EMFILE`. Refactor the
open calls to use a context manager (or an explicit close in
`finally`), add a test that verifies no descriptor is leaked across
one good import + one failing import, and run the suite.

items.title: rounding error in invoice total
items.prompt:
`app/billing/invoice.py` totals line items with floating-point
arithmetic and produces values like `0.30000000000000004` for what
should be `0.30`, which then fails downstream equality checks against
the expected total. Switch the totalling code to `decimal.Decimal`
with an explicit quantisation (or an integer-cents representation),
add a regression test that asserts exact equality on the affected
case, and run the test suite.

items.title: cache invalidation skipped on update
items.prompt:
`app/services/profile_cache.py` writes through to the database on
`update_profile` but forgets to invalidate the in-memory cache,
so subsequent reads return stale data until the cache TTL expires.
Wire the invalidation in (delete or set the new value), add a
regression test that asserts a read after an update reflects the
new value, and run the test suite.

items.title: missing pagination in admin user list
items.prompt:
`/api/admin/users` returns the full user table in one response.
On a database with 50k users this triggers OOM in the JSON
serialiser and times out the upstream proxy. Add cursor-based
pagination (`?cursor=`, `?limit=`) with a sane default and max
limit, add a regression test covering first-page, mid-stream, and
last-page behaviour, and run the test suite.

items.title: integer overflow in metric aggregator
items.prompt:
`app/metrics/aggregator.py` accumulates per-request byte counts
into a 32-bit `int32` numpy column, so high-traffic days wrap and
produce negative totals on the dashboard. Switch the accumulator
to a width that cannot overflow on realistic loads (`int64` or
Python's arbitrary-precision int), add a regression test that
feeds enough volume to trigger the old overflow and asserts the
positive total, and run the test suite.

items.title: incorrect json escape in webhook signer
items.prompt:
`app/webhooks/signer.py` builds the canonical request body for HMAC
signing by `json.dumps(payload)` with default settings, but the
upstream verifier expects `ensure_ascii=False` and sorted keys,
so any payload containing non-ASCII characters or unsorted keys
fails signature verification. Align the signer with the verifier's
canonical form, add a regression test using a payload with a CJK
string and out-of-order keys, and run the test suite.

items.title: retry loop swallows non-retriable errors
items.prompt:
`app/clients/payment_client.py` wraps every API call in a generic
retry-on-exception loop, so non-retriable errors (validation errors,
hard 4xx) are retried until the budget is exhausted and only then
surfaced as a generic timeout. Restrict retries to genuinely
transient classes (network errors, 5xx, explicit retry signals),
re-raise other exceptions immediately, add a regression test that
asserts a 422 is raised on the first attempt, and run the test
suite.

items.title: regex catastrophic backtracking in log parser
items.prompt:
`app/parsers/log_parser.py` uses a regex like `(.*),(.*),(.*)`
with greedy `.*` to split log lines, which exhibits catastrophic
backtracking on pathological inputs and stalls log ingestion for
minutes. Replace the regex with an unambiguous form (anchored,
non-greedy, or just `str.split`), add a regression test that
parses a known-pathological line within a hard timeout, and run
the test suite.

items.title: env var read at import time
items.prompt:
`app/config/settings.py` reads `os.environ["DATABASE_URL"]` at
module import time and assigns it to a module-level constant, so
tests that monkeypatch the env var after import always see the
original value and silently exercise the wrong database. Defer
the env read into a function (or a lazy property), add a
regression test that monkeypatches the env var, calls the
accessor, and asserts the new value is reflected, then run the
test suite.

items.title: silent UnicodeDecodeError on log ingest
items.prompt:
`app/ingest/file_reader.py` reads incoming log files with
`open(path).read()` (default platform encoding), so files written
by upstream services in UTF-8 with non-ASCII bytes raise
`UnicodeDecodeError` on Latin-1 hosts and the ingest worker
silently skips the rest of the batch. Pin the open call to
`encoding="utf-8"` with `errors="replace"` (or another deliberate
policy), add a regression test that feeds a UTF-8 sample
containing CJK characters and asserts the contents round-trip,
and run the test suite.

items.title: thundering herd on cache miss
items.prompt:
`app/cache/loader.py` recomputes an expensive value on every cache
miss, but when the cached entry expires under traffic, dozens of
concurrent requests all see the miss and recompute simultaneously,
hammering the upstream database. Add single-flight de-duplication
(per-key lock, future, or `cachetools.cached`-style coalescing)
so only one request recomputes and the others await the result,
add a regression test that fires concurrent gets on a cold key
and asserts the loader was invoked exactly once, and run the
test suite.

items.title: TOCTOU race in temp file creation
items.prompt:
`app/utils/tempfile_helper.py` checks `os.path.exists(path)` and then
calls `open(path, "w")` if missing — a classic TOCTOU window where
two concurrent processes both pass the check and one overwrites the
other's data. Switch to `os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)`
(or `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile`), add a regression test that races
two threads and asserts only one succeeds, and run the test suite.

items.title: connection pool exhaustion under load
items.prompt:
`app/db/pool.py` opens a new psycopg2 connection per request and never
closes it on the success path, so under sustained traffic the database
rejects connections after pool_max_size is hit. Wire the connections
through a context manager (or pool.putconn in finally), add a
regression test that fires N+1 sequential requests where N=pool_max
and asserts none fail with «connection limit», and run the test suite.

items.title: timezone confusion in scheduled job
items.prompt:
`app/jobs/scheduler.py` mixes `datetime.now()` (naive, server-local) and
`datetime.now(timezone.utc)` when comparing the next-run time against
«now», so jobs fire 8 hours early or late depending on host TZ. Pin
every datetime path to UTC-aware, add a regression test that simulates
a non-UTC host and asserts the next-run computation is correct, and
run the test suite.

items.title: missing transaction rollback on exception
items.prompt:
`app/services/order_service.py` performs three sequential SQL writes
(insert order, decrement inventory, charge customer) but on exception
in the middle write the order row stays committed while inventory
doesn't decrement. Wrap the operation in a single transaction with
proper rollback semantics, add a regression test that injects a
failure on step 2 and asserts no order row remains, and run the
test suite.

items.title: deadlock between two service locks
items.prompt:
`app/services/transfer_service.py` acquires lock_account_a then
lock_account_b for transfers from A to B, but a parallel transfer
from B to A acquires lock_account_b then lock_account_a, producing
classic AB-BA deadlock under concurrent transfers. Enforce a stable
lock order (smallest account_id first), add a regression test that
spins up two concurrent transfers in opposite directions and asserts
both complete, and run the test suite.

items.title: SQL injection in raw query construction
items.prompt:
`app/api/search.py` builds a SQL query with f-string interpolation
of `request.args["q"]`, so a crafted query argument can drop tables
or exfiltrate data. Replace the raw query with parameterised
placeholders (psycopg2 `%s` or SQLAlchemy text bindings), add a
regression test that includes a payload like `'; DROP TABLE users; --`
and asserts the table still exists, and run the test suite.

items.title: missing input validation on API boundary
items.prompt:
`app/api/upload.py` accepts a `size` query parameter and passes it
unchecked to `bytearray(size)`, so a request with `size=10000000000`
attempts a 10 GB allocation and crashes the worker. Add bounded
validation (`int` cast + range check 0..MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES), return
HTTP 400 on out-of-range, add a regression test for both valid and
oversize inputs, and run the test suite.

items.title: improper error swallowing in async task
items.prompt:
`app/workers/email_worker.py` catches `Exception` in the main loop
and `pass`es silently, so transient SMTP errors that should retry
and persistent config errors that should page on-call both vanish
with no log entry. Replace the bare except with: log the exception
with traceback, classify retriable vs fatal, requeue retriable
with exponential backoff, raise fatal. Add a regression test that
injects each class and asserts the right path fires, and run the
test suite.

items.title: stale field in serialiser after model update
items.prompt:
`app/api/users.py` serialises the User model to JSON via a hand-rolled
`to_dict` that hasn't been updated since the `email_verified_at`
column was added in migration 0023, so the API silently omits the
new field. Switch the serialiser to introspect declared columns
(`Model.__table__.columns`) so future schema changes propagate
automatically, add a regression test asserting `email_verified_at`
appears in the response, and run the test suite.

items.title: signed-token verification skips expiry check
items.prompt:
`app/auth/token.py` verifies a JWT signature but accidentally drops
the `verify_exp=True` flag when refactoring to PyJWT v2 — so expired
tokens still authenticate. Restore expiry verification, add a
regression test that crafts a token with `exp` in the past and
asserts `decode()` raises `ExpiredSignatureError`, and run the
test suite.

items.title: idempotency missing on payment intent
items.prompt:
`app/api/payments.py` creates a Stripe payment intent on every POST
with no idempotency key, so a client retry on a flaky network double-
charges the customer. Pass an idempotency key derived from
`(user_id, order_id, amount)` to Stripe's `PaymentIntent.create`,
add a regression test that calls the endpoint twice with the same
body and asserts only one charge, and run the test suite.

items.title: missing rate-limit on login endpoint
items.prompt:
`app/api/auth.py` exposes `/login` with no rate-limiting, allowing
unbounded credential stuffing from one IP. Add a per-IP sliding-window
limiter (e.g. 10 attempts per 15 minutes) backed by Redis or an
in-memory store with TTL, return HTTP 429 when exceeded, add a
regression test that fires N+1 attempts and asserts the last is
rate-limited, and run the test suite.

items.title: regex matches across newlines unintentionally
items.prompt:
`app/parsers/markdown.py` extracts code-fence blocks with the regex
`r"```(.+?)```"` and the `re.DOTALL` flag inadvertently makes `.+?`
span across paragraph breaks, so two adjacent fences merge into
one. Anchor the match per-line (drop DOTALL, use `[\s\S]` only inside
fences, or split on the closing marker first), add a regression test
with two adjacent fences and assert two distinct matches, and run
the test suite.

items.title: missing CSRF token check on state-changing endpoint
items.prompt:
`app/api/profile.py` exposes a POST `/profile/email` endpoint with
no CSRF token verification, so a malicious site can change a
victim's email by submitting a form while the user is logged in.
Add CSRF token validation (compare cookie + header, time-constant
comparison), return HTTP 403 on mismatch, add a regression test
for both valid + missing token paths, and run the test suite.

items.title: floating-point comparison without epsilon
items.prompt:
`app/services/inventory.py` checks `if running_total == target_total`
to verify reconciliation, but accumulated floating-point error from
thousands of small additions makes the equality fail even when the
totals match to 8 decimal places. Use `math.isclose(a, b, abs_tol=1e-6)`
(or pre-multiply by a power of 10 to integer-cents), add a regression
test that runs a 10000-step accumulation and asserts the check
succeeds, and run the test suite.

items.title: FastAPI todo with auth
items.prompt:
Build a FastAPI service skeleton with: JWT-based authentication (login,
signup, password hashing with bcrypt), a User model + CRUD, a Todo model
+ CRUD scoped to the authenticated user, alembic migrations, and pytest
coverage for the auth flow. Include a README with run instructions.

items.title: Express + Mongoose REST API with auth
items.prompt:
Build an Express + Mongoose service: User and Post models, JWT-based
auth (register, login, refresh), CRUD for posts scoped to the
authenticated user, validation via zod, environment config via
dotenv, jest tests covering at least the auth flow + post creation.
Include a README with run + test instructions.

items.title: Go gin URL shortener
items.prompt:
Build a URL shortener service with Gin + sqlx + Postgres: POST /shorten
to create a short code, GET /:code to redirect, in-memory rate limit
(10 req/min per IP), graceful shutdown, structured logging via slog.
Include go test coverage for the create + redirect paths and a README.

items.title: Next.js 14 admin dashboard with Drizzle
items.prompt:
Scaffold a Next.js 14 (app router) admin dashboard: Drizzle ORM with
Postgres, server actions for CRUD on a Customer table, a /customers
page listing rows with pagination, /customers/[id] for edit, login
page guarded by NextAuth credentials provider, basic Tailwind layout.
Include a vitest test for the create-customer server action and a
README with run instructions.

items.title: Flask + SQLAlchemy blog with comments
items.prompt:
Build a Flask blog: SQLAlchemy models for User / Post / Comment,
Flask-Login session auth, Markdown post rendering via markdown2,
CRUD for posts (author-scoped) + nested comments, alembic migrations,
pytest covering the login + post creation flow. Include a README
with run + test instructions.

items.title: GraphQL server with Apollo + Prisma
items.prompt:
Build an Apollo GraphQL server backed by Prisma + Postgres: schema
for User and Project (one-to-many), queries (`me`, `project`,
`projects`), mutations (`createProject`, `updateProject`,
`deleteProject`), JWT auth middleware on the GraphQL context, a
vitest test that runs `createProject` against an in-memory store.
Include README.

items.title: Rust axum REST API with sqlx
items.prompt:
Build a Rust axum service with sqlx + Postgres: a Customer resource
with POST/GET/PUT/DELETE, JWT auth via tower middleware, structured
config via figment, error handling via thiserror + custom IntoResponse,
a single integration test using reqwest against a mock pool. Include
Cargo.toml + README.

items.title: Django REST Framework polls API
items.prompt:
Scaffold a Django + DRF polls service: Poll and Choice models, viewset
for CRUD on polls, voting endpoint that increments choice count
atomically (select_for_update), TokenAuthentication, throttling
(10 votes/min/user), pytest-django covering the vote flow, and
a manage.py + settings module. Include a README.

items.title: NestJS microservice with TypeORM
items.prompt:
Build a NestJS microservice for a billing module: TypeORM with Postgres,
Invoice and LineItem entities (one-to-many cascade), controller for
create/list/get, JwtGuard on the controller, validation pipes via
class-validator, jest e2e test that creates an invoice with two line
items and asserts the total. Include README.

items.title: Python Click CLI for log analysis
items.prompt:
Build a Click-based CLI tool `loganalyze` with subcommands: `parse`
(read JSON-lines stdin and emit a per-event-type histogram),
`tail-errors` (stream events from a file and print only level=error),
`summary` (compute p50/p95 latency from `duration_ms`). Include
pyproject.toml with the entry point, pytest covering each subcommand
using a fixture file, README with example commands.

items.title: Tornado WebSocket chat server
items.prompt:
Build a Tornado WebSocket chat server: a single /ws endpoint, in-memory
room registry, broadcast on `say`, presence via `join`/`leave` system
messages, persistent message history (last 100) loaded on connect from
Redis, structured JSON messages, pytest covering one client sending +
one client receiving via tornado.testing.AsyncHTTPTestCase. Include
README.

items.title: Spring Boot REST API with JPA
items.prompt:
Scaffold a Spring Boot 3 service: ProductController + ProductService
+ ProductRepository (JPA), Bean Validation on POST/PUT, Spring
Security with HTTP Basic for write endpoints, Flyway migration for
the products table, JUnit 5 + Spring MockMvc test for create + list.
Include build.gradle.kts and README.

items.title: Vue 3 + Pinia frontend with API mocking
items.prompt:
Scaffold a Vue 3 (composition API) frontend: Pinia store for a Todo
list, components TodoList + TodoForm, fetch wrapper with msw mocking
for tests, vitest + @vue/test-utils covering store mutations + form
submission, vite config + tsconfig. Include README.

items.title: gRPC service with protobuf-defined contract
items.prompt:
Build a Python gRPC service for a Notes API: protobuf with Note message
+ NoteService (Create / Get / List / Delete), generated stubs via
grpcio-tools, in-memory store with thread-safe access, server entry
point that listens on port 50051, pytest test that uses an in-process
channel to call Create + Get and asserts equality. Include README.

items.title: Celery task queue with Redis broker
items.prompt:
Scaffold a Celery worker setup: Redis broker + result backend, three
tasks (`send_email`, `generate_thumbnail`, `recompute_metrics`) with
retry policy, beat schedule for the metrics task, pytest using
`celery.contrib.pytest` to run tasks eagerly and assert state
transitions. Include README with worker + beat run commands.

items.title: React + Vite todo SPA with localStorage
items.prompt:
Scaffold a React 18 + Vite todo SPA: components TodoList,
TodoItem, TodoForm; useReducer for state; localStorage persistence
via useEffect; CSS Modules or Tailwind; vitest + @testing-library/react
covering add/toggle/delete actions; deploy-ready vite config. Include
package.json, tsconfig, README.

items.title: Kafka consumer + producer pipeline
items.prompt:
Build a Kafka producer / consumer pipeline using confluent-kafka:
producer reads JSON-line input from stdin and emits to topic
`events`, consumer subscribes to `events` and aggregates by
`event_type` into a dict, exposes a /metrics HTTP endpoint via
Flask. Pytest using kafka-python's MockProducer for the
producer-side path + a dict-driven fake for the consumer side.
Include docker-compose.yml + README.

items.title: SvelteKit blog with markdown frontmatter
items.prompt:
Scaffold a SvelteKit blog: file-system routing for `/blog`, `/blog/[slug]`,
posts loaded from local `content/*.md` with frontmatter parsing
(gray-matter), markdown-it rendering, RSS feed at `/feed.xml`, and
a simple Tailwind layout. Include vitest test for the post-list
loader, package.json, svelte.config.js, README.

items.title: Async FastAPI + httpx external API aggregator
items.prompt:
Build an async FastAPI service that aggregates data from three
external HTTP APIs concurrently using httpx.AsyncClient: a
`/dashboard/{user_id}` endpoint that fetches profile from
api-A, recent activity from api-B, and recommendations from api-C
in parallel and merges the results. Include retry/timeout policy,
pytest with respx mocks for the three upstreams, and asserts
end-to-end response < 2x slowest upstream wall.

items.title: ETL pipeline with prefect 2 + S3 + DuckDB
items.prompt:
Build a Prefect 2 ETL flow that reads gzipped CSVs from a local
`data/` directory (mocked S3), normalises columns into a target
schema, runs row-level validations with pydantic, writes to a
DuckDB file, emits a summary task with row counts. Include a
pytest that runs the flow against fixture CSVs and asserts the
DuckDB output. Include README with run + test commands.
